build.sig: build(self, /, all_vectors, doc_ids, doc_offsets, n_fine=256, n_coarse=32, max_degree=32, ef_construction=200, max_kmeans_iter=30, ctop_r=3) unbound builtins.GemSegment method
build.doc:
Build a sealed GEM segment from document vectors.

Args:
  all_vectors: (N, D) float32 matrix of all token vectors
  doc_ids: external document IDs
  doc_offsets: (start, end) index pairs into all_vectors per document
  n_fine: number of fine centroids (codebook size)
  n_coarse: number of coarse clusters
  max_degree: max neighbors per graph node (M parameter)
  ef_construction: beam width during graph construction
  max_kmeans_iter: k-means iteration limit
  ctop_r: number of top coarse clusters per document

dim.doc: Return the vector dimensionality of the segment.

inject_shortcuts.sig: inject_shortcuts(self, /, training_pairs, max_shortcuts_per_node=4) unbound builtins.GemSegment method
inject_shortcuts.doc:
Inject semantic shortcuts from training pairs.
training_pairs: list of (query_vectors_flat, positive_doc_internal_id)

is_ready.doc: Return True if the segment is built and ready for queries.

n_docs.doc: Return the number of documents in the segment.

n_edges.doc: Return the total number of edges in the graph.

n_nodes.doc: Return the total number of nodes in the graph.

search.sig: search(self, /, query_vectors, k=10, ef=100, n_probes=4, enable_shortcuts=False) unbound builtins.GemSegment method
search.doc:
Search the sealed segment for nearest neighbors to a multi-vector query.

Returns: list of (doc_id, qch_proxy_score) tuples, sorted best-first

total_shortcuts.doc: Return the total number of shortcut edges.

avg_degree.doc: Return the average node degree in the mutable graph.

build.sig: build(self, /, all_vectors, doc_ids, doc_offsets, n_fine=256, n_coarse=32, max_degree=32, ef_construction=200, max_kmeans_iter=30, ctop_r=3, n_probes=4) unbound builtins.PyMutableGemSegment method
build.doc: Build a mutable segment from a seed batch: trains codebook + builds initial graph.

compact.doc: Compact the segment: rebuild graph without deleted nodes.

delete.doc: Soft-delete a document by external ID. Returns True if found.

delete_ratio.doc: Return the fraction of deleted nodes (0.0 to 1.0).

insert.sig: insert(self, /, vectors, doc_id) unbound builtins.PyMutableGemSegment method
insert.doc: Insert a single document (multi-vector) into the mutable graph.

is_ready.doc: Return True if the mutable segment is built and ready.

memory_bytes.doc: Estimated memory usage of the mutable segment in bytes.

n_edges.doc: Return the total number of edges.

n_live.doc: Return the number of non-deleted documents.

n_nodes.doc: Return the total number of nodes (including deleted).

quality_score.doc: Quality score: 1.0 = same as initial build, < 1.0 = degraded.

search.sig: search(self, /, query_vectors, k=10, ef=100, _n_probes=4) unbound builtins.PyMutableGemSegment method

upsert.sig: upsert(self, /, vectors, doc_id) unbound builtins.PyMutableGemSegment method
upsert.doc: Upsert: delete old version (if exists) then insert new.

add_documents.sig: add_documents(self, /, new_vectors, new_doc_ids, doc_offsets) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
add_documents.doc: Add new documents to an existing router.

build.sig: build(self, /, all_vectors, doc_ids, doc_offsets, n_fine=256, n_coarse=32, max_kmeans_iter=30, ctop_r=3) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
build.doc:
Build the router from a matrix of all document vectors.

Args:
  all_vectors: (N, D) float32 matrix of all token/prototype vectors
  doc_ids: list of document IDs (length = n_docs)
  doc_offsets: list of (start, end) tuples indexing into all_vectors for each doc
  n_fine: number of fine centroids (codebook size)
  n_coarse: number of coarse clusters
  max_kmeans_iter: k-means iteration limit
  ctop_r: number of top coarse clusters per document

compute_query_profile.sig: compute_query_profile(self, /, query_vectors, n_probes=4) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
compute_query_profile.doc:
Compute query centroid IDs and C_top.

Returns: (centroid_ids: list[int], ctop: list[int])

get_cluster_entries.sig: get_cluster_entries(self, /, query_vectors, n_probes=4) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
get_cluster_entries.doc:
Get HNSW entry point hints for multi-entry traversal.

Returns doc IDs of cluster representatives for the query's most relevant clusters.

is_ready.doc: Check if the router is built and ready for queries.

n_fine.doc: Number of fine centroids in the codebook.

query_cluster_overlaps.sig: query_cluster_overlaps(self, /, query_vectors, n_probes=4) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
query_cluster_overlaps.doc:
Get per-document cluster overlap with a query's C_top.

Returns: list of (doc_id, overlap_count)

route_query.sig: route_query(self, /, query_vectors, n_probes=4, max_candidates=500) unbound builtins.PyGemRouter method
route_query.doc:
Route a query to candidate documents.

Args:
  query_vectors: (Q, D) float32 matrix of query token vectors
  n_probes: number of coarse clusters to probe
  max_candidates: max number of candidates to return

Returns:
  list of (doc_id, proxy_score) tuples, sorted best-first
